evidence.description: Edge case IDs EC-S-005 and EC-S-010 are each defined twice with mutually inconsistent meanings between REQ-FILTER-003 inline edge cases and the §4.1 Edge Case Catalog table.

sortByUpdatedAt has two contradictory signatures: §1.5 line 65 declares it as (direction, a, b) → number, but §2 pseudocode line 149 calls it as sortByUpdatedAt(sortDir, noteMetadata) — a curried-comparator factory. Both cannot hold.
DomainSnapshotReceived pseudocode references an undefined identifier reconstructedState and never spells out how allNoteIds, noteMetadata, editingStatus, etc. are derived from the new snapshot.

Architecture line 63-66 declares sortByUpdatedAt(direction, noteMetadata): comparator (matches behavioral-spec §2 pseudocode) but contradicts behavioral-spec §1.5 line 65. The architecture also maps PROP-FILTER-022 (rapid keystrokes) to EC-S-001 (empty string dispatched), which is the wrong edge case.

Iteration 2 enumerates Unicode behavior (ASCII fold only, toLocaleLowerCase prohibited), pathological inputs (control chars, 10k length, RTL, regex metacharacters, repeat Esc), and debounce-vs-snapshot race (EC-C-007, EC-T-004) explicitly. Accessibility labels are pinned at REQ-FILTER-013.

PROP-FILTER-010 now explicitly limits the case-insensitive equivalence claim to ASCII printable strings and only requires no-throw for arbitrary Unicode. PROP-FILTER-005 frames feedReducer totality over arbitrary FeedAction including 0..10000-character SearchApplied.query. fast-check generators are pinned to these boundaries.

Done definition items 1-10 are now artifact-verifiable (no Phase 3/Phase 6 self-reference) — that part is resolved. However, items 7 (REQ-FILTER-001..017 ↔ test) and 8 (PROP-FILTER-001..025 ↔ proof) cannot be evaluated when (a) duplicate edge case IDs and (b) underspecified DomainSnapshotReceived state reconstruction leave tests with no canonical target. Convergence target is unstable until those resolve.

previousIterationFindings.FIND-SPEC-FILTER-001: RESOLVED — operational flow pseudocode in §2 disambiguates dispatch path
previousIterationFindings.FIND-SPEC-FILTER-002: RESOLVED — §1.5 places applyFilterOrSearch in effectful shell and explicitly excludes it from this feature
previousIterationFindings.FIND-SPEC-FILTER-003: RESOLVED — searchPredicate explicitly placed in pure core; SearchInputChanged removed from FeedAction
previousIterationFindings.FIND-SPEC-FILTER-004: RESOLVED — FilterApplied/FilterCleared documented as backward-compatible, unused by this feature
previousIterationFindings.FIND-SPEC-FILTER-005: RESOLVED — debounce timer ownership pinned to SearchInput.svelte (§1.5, §2)
previousIterationFindings.FIND-SPEC-FILTER-006: RESOLVED — REQ-FILTER-005 explicitly states toLowerCase ASCII-fold only; toLocaleLowerCase prohibited; PROP-FILTER-010 narrows correctness claim to ASCII
previousIterationFindings.FIND-SPEC-FILTER-007: RESOLVED — EC-C-007 and EC-T-004 enumerate debounce-vs-snapshot and toggle-vs-debounce races
previousIterationFindings.FIND-SPEC-FILTER-009: RESOLVED — REQ-FILTER-017 enumerates pathological inputs (regex metachars, RTL, 10k length, control chars)
previousIterationFindings.FIND-SPEC-FILTER-010: RESOLVED — REQ-FILTER-013 pins aria-labels and Tab order; PROP-FILTER-025 covers verification
previousIterationFindings.FIND-SPEC-FILTER-011: PARTIALLY RESOLVED — Done items 1-10 are artifact-verifiable, but new defects (FIND-012, 013, 015) keep the convergence target unstable
